title: Python practice question (row of the `questions` table)
description: Contract for inserting Python questions — via the admin panel, POST /api/admin/add-questions, or directly into Supabase (e.g. through an MCP server). Derived from the live data export (public/questions_rows-1.csv). Mirrors the runtime validator in src/lib/question-schema.ts — keep the two in sync. CRITICAL for direct inserts: multi-line code in `question`/`answer` must contain real newline characters. Never flatten newlines to spaces (produces invalid Python) and never store the literal two-character sequence \n.

id.description: Unique id. Convention: tier initial + zero-padded number — S=simple, I=intermediate, H=hard, E=expert (e.g. "S031").

language.description: Defaults to "python" in the DB; may be omitted on insert.

question.description: The prompt. For fill_in_the_blank / spot_the_bug (and usually prediction types): prose, then a blank line, then the code block — splitPrompt() uses the first blank line to pre-fill the editor.

answer.description: write_the_code: full solution code. fill_in_the_blank: the completed code (blanks filled). spot_the_bug: the fixed code. output_prediction / what_is_the_result: the EXACT output string the learner must type.

alternative_answer.description: Optional second accepted answer/output. Null if none.

explanation.description: Shown after solving — why the answer works.

expected_output.description: The exact stdout produced by running the solution. REQUIRED for write_the_code / fill_in_the_blank / spot_the_bug: the checker (src/lib/answer-check.ts) compares the learner's stdout against this — without it the check falls back to the solution source code and every submission is marked incorrect. For prediction types it may be null (falls back to `answer`).

created_at.description: Set by the DB default — omit on insert.
